ddls:
CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., Forecast, Sales) */,
  "source_path" TEXT /* Path to the data source (e.g., S3 bucket path) */,
  "source_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which data is ingested from this source (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "source_format" TEXT /* Format of the data (e.g., CSV, JSON, Excel) */,
  "last_ingestion_date" TEXT /* Last date data was ingested from this source */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the contact person for the data source */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email of the contact person for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_layers" (
  "layer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data layer */,
  "layer_name" TEXT /* Name of the data layer (e.g., LZ, RAW) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data layer */,
  "layer_type" TEXT /* Type of data layer (e.g., Landing Zone, Raw, Cleaned) */,
  "layer_retention_period" INTEGER /* Number of days data should be retained in this layer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("layer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "validation_rules" (
  "rule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation rule */,
  "layer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data layer where the rule is applied */,
  "rule_name" TEXT /* Name of the validation rule */,
  "rule_description" TEXT /* Description of the validation rule */,
  "rule_query" TEXT /* SQL query or script to execute the validation rule */,
  "expected_result" TEXT /* Expected result of the validation rule */,
  "rule_priority" INTEGER /* Priority level of the validation rule */,
  "rule_severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the validation rule */,
  "rule_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which the rule should be applied */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rule_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data source where the file originates */,
  "layer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data layer where the file is stored */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the file in the S3 bucket */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the file in MB */,
  "ingestion_date" TEXT /* Date the file was ingested */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the file (e.g., ingested, validated, transformed) */,
  "file_extension" TEXT /* File extension (e.g., .csv, .xlsx) */,
  "file_checksum" TEXT /* Checksum of the file to ensure data integrity */,
  "file_format" TEXT /* Format of the file (e.g., CSV, JSON, Excel) */,
  "file_encoding" TEXT /* Encoding of the file (e.g., UTF-8) */,
  "file_version" TEXT /* Version of the file (e.g., v1.0) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_layer_id FOREIGN KEY ("layer_id") REFERENCES data_layers ("layer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_files_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sets" (
  "set_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data set */,
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the file the data set belongs to */,
  "set_name" TEXT /* Name of the data set */,
  "set_description" TEXT /* Description of the data set */,
  "set_date" TEXT /* Date the data set was created */,
  "set_size" REAL /* Size of the data set in MB */,
  "set_type" TEXT /* Type of the data set (e.g., forecast, sales) */,
  "set_source" TEXT /* Source of the data set (e.g., external, internal) */,
  "set_status" TEXT /* Status of the data set (e.g., active, archived) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("set_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_sets_file_id FOREIGN KEY ("file_id") REFERENCES data_files ("file_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data engineer, data scientist, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "user_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "user_status" TEXT /* Status of the user (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "user_last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "user_created_date" TEXT /* Date the user account was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
